---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Explore real world cases of adaptive governance policies being implemented to address environmental problems
]]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Which of the four policy design options is the "right" choice for addressing your environmental problem?

- Analyze the pros and cons of each option for your chosen problem, and

- Support all claims with evidence
]

???

Keep your eye on the next target: Paper 2.

- In that paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

### Any questions on the prompt?




---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: Adaptive Governance Cases**]

.size45[
Find a real world example of .textblue[**this approach**] being used to .textblue[**successfully**] address an environmental problem .textblue[**similar to the one in your project**].
]

.size45[
.textblue[**Present as an argument**]: This case shows that addressing environmental problem X can be done successfully using "green" subsidies.
]

???

### Does everybody have an argument to present and discuss today?

<br>

Let's review all the cases for APA format.



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size45[
This case shows that using adaptive governance we can address environmental problem X.

- What specific, narrow aspect of your problem does this approach focus on?

- Which stakeholders does it target? What does it require them to do?

- What institutions support this approach?
]

???

Take five minutes to reflect on your argument and get ready to present it to other people as answers to these three questions.

### Questions on this?

Get to it!

<br>

Ok, everybody stand up and welcome to our environmental policy cocktail party.

We will now take 25 minutes to mingle, present our cases and hear everybody elses' cases.

I don't want to see groups of three!

- Your job is to interact in pairs.
- I want focused groups each hearing one case and presenting one case.

### Questions?

Go!

*You mingle too.*
- This was super fun and you got to help steer students to being more specific, more precise



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

???

Ok, at this point you've heard MANY different cases and hopefully gotten some time to reflect on your own case argument.

Now let's try to extract lessons from all of them.

- Everybody form groups of (three? four?) and spread out around the room.

Groups compare and contrast each set of answers and help the class extract generalizable lessons to help us evaluate green subsidies



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
1) How specifically do these policies target the problem? 
]

???

Ok, groups, first prompt.

Any environmental problem is likely to be large and multi-faceted, but I'm willing to bet these policies are highly targeted.

### From what you just heard, how specifically do these policies target the problem?
- *Report back and discuss*

<br>

### Based on these elements, what are the characteristics of an environmental problem that lends itself to using an adaptive governance approach? What kinds do not?

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Problem Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
2) What kinds of stakeholders do these policies target? 
]

???

Ok, groups, second prompt.

### From what you just heard, who specifically do these policies target? How does it target them?
- *Report back and discuss*

<br>

### Based on these elements, what are the characteristics of a stakeholder that lends itself to using adaptive governance? What kinds do not?

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Stakeholder Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
3) What kinds of local institutions exist to support and/or reinforce these policies? 
]

???

Ok, groups, final prompt.

### From what you just heard, what institutions do these policies rely on? Are the policies supported by institutional actors or built into established processes?
- *Report back and discuss*

<br>

### Based on these elements, what are the kinds of institutional needs to make adaptive governance work?

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Institutional Support'

- ?


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-fishing.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: bottom
class: top, center, inverse

.textwhite[.size50[**Best advice / things to remember when designing adaptive governance policies?**]]

???

*ON BOARD*

Let's work together to build a policy cheat sheet on the board.

If you were designing a local control regime to address an environmental problem what would you need to keep in mind?

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size70[**Next Week**]

<br>

.size70[**Final Papers!**]
